body.title: Terminally Ill Adults (End of Life) Bill — Second Reading
body.$mode_note: A Commons division is a PUBLIC, NAMED, recorded vote — the deliberate OPPOSITE of the citizen ballot's receipt-freeness (§3.2/T4). That is correct for a representative body: an MP's vote is public so constituents can hold them to it, whereas a citizen's ballot is secret so no one can coerce it. The kernel's decision service (SVC-2) covers BOTH modes; a parliamentary division is the public-named profile.
body.$provenance_note: This entry REPRESENTS a real, already-recorded outcome; it does not re-derive it cryptographically. There is no homomorphic tally proof for a 2024 lobby division — the authority for the numbers is Hansard, cited below. Do not read the sig as real crypto (see $sig_note).
body.$sig_note: The sig below is a placeholder — this corpus is a representation of public record, not a signed Parliament deployment.
